sourceMovie.title: Rango
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Rango (2011) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: An American Tail: Fievel Goes West
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rango clicked for you, An American Tail: Fievel Goes West (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rango clicked for you, Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rango?
faq.answer: Try An American Tail: Fievel Goes West, Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron, Puss in Boots—each shares DNA with Rango while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rango?
faq.answer: Rango (2011) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family, Western, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rango worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
